name: Healthcare Clinical AI
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: Clinical decision support — evidence-based treatment recommendations, differential diagnosis generation, lab result interpretation, medication interaction checks, and clinical note summarization

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput for hospital-wide deployment, guaranteed SLA

services.name: Azure Health Data Services (FHIR)
services.purpose: Clinical data layer — FHIR R4 patient records, observations, conditions, medications, allergies, procedures, diagnostic reports, and care plans

services.name: Azure AI Search
services.purpose: Clinical knowledge retrieval — semantic search over medical literature, clinical guidelines, drug databases, formularies, and institutional protocols for evidence-based recommendations

services.name: Azure AI Content Safety
services.purpose: Clinical output safety — ensures AI recommendations don't contain harmful guidance, validates clinical appropriateness, detects hallucinated medical facts, and blocks dangerous medication suggestions

services.name: Azure App Service
services.purpose: Clinical decision support application — clinician-facing web portal, patient context viewer, recommendation display, human-in-the-loop approval workflow, and audit dashboard

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Decision audit trail — every AI recommendation, clinician action (accept/reject/modify), patient context snapshot, evidence citations, and compliance audit records

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: PHI encryption keys, FHIR API credentials, OpenAI keys, Content Safety keys, SSL certificates, SMART on FHIR client secrets

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Clinical AI observability — recommendation acceptance rate, response latency, safety rejection rates, evidence citation accuracy, clinician feedback metrics

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for routine tasks (clinical note summarization, lab value normalization) and reserve GPT-4o for complex differential diagnosis and multi-factor treatment recommendations — reduces AI costs by 60%
Cache clinical guideline lookups by condition code (ICD-10) — guidelines change infrequently, so a 24-hour cache eliminates redundant AI Search queries for the same conditions
Implement FHIR resource caching at the application layer — patient demographics and active medication lists are read 100x more than written, so a 5-minute cache reduces FHIR API costs significantly
Use AI Search semantic ranker only for complex medical queries — simple keyword lookups (drug names, ICD codes) can use basic search at zero semantic ranker cost
Batch Content Safety evaluations for non-urgent recommendations — real-time screening for acute care, batched hourly for routine wellness recommendations
Use Cosmos DB serverless for dev/staging and provisioned only for production — clinical audit data is write-heavy but read-infrequent except during compliance audits
